reviews.review_title: Township Tale is Peak VR RPG
reviews.review_description:
A Township Tale delivers one of the most immersive and rewarding RPG experiences in VR. The game absolutely shines in its crafting depth—you don’t just craft weapons, you hand-build them. From the handle, guard, pommel, and blade of a sword to everyday tools like bowls, ladles, buckets, lanterns, bags, and more, everything reflects the player’s choices. Your gear truly shows your identity and playstyle.
Exploration is another standout. There are many places to discover, though it’s worth noting that you’ll experience the full potential of exploration on the free PCVR version. Highlights include the Climbing Tower, a 1–2 hour journey that unlocks the ability to climb most surfaces in the world. This opens up new loot paths, vantage points, and eventually leads you through encounters with nearly every enemy in the game—all while enjoying the game’s charming cartoon art style, either solo or with friends.
The mines feel endless in the best way, offering a wide variety of metals and alloys. Each material comes with its own buffs and debuffs, encouraging experimentation and progression while keeping the grind engaging rather than tedious.
Where the game stumbles is not in design—but in development support.
When I first started playing, the community was amazing. Servers were packed, queues were common, and the world felt alive. Over time, updates became more and more distant. Recently, it came to light that the developers are working on a second project (often referred to as Reven), and most—if not all—of the A Township Tale development team appears to have been laid off. As a result, the game feels largely halted in terms of meaningful updates.
If you buy the game now on Quest or PCVR, expect the Tutorial World to be the main hub—where new and veteran players advertise servers, help each other, and try to keep the game alive. Many older servers that were once constantly full are now abandoned, collecting dust and cobwebs. Some barely hold together. You might even run into me and a few friends actively trying to grow a server and help rebuild what’s left of a once-thriving community.
Final Verdict:
A Township Tale is still an incredible VR RPG and absolutely worth experiencing—but it’s also a reminder of how poor developer support can leave a masterpiece in limbo. Buy it for what it is, not for what it could have been.

First I LOVE this game, no.1 favourite on the quest. I absolutely adore how well this game was made and the amount of endless possibilities of doing what you want when you want.
But the thing is, the possibilities of having fun can be endless but can still have more things to enjoy. Which brings me to the main purpose of why I can’t get enough of this game.
The forest is an epic biome and has a LOT more content to add to the game to make it more appealing to other quest users. 
As far as I am concerned, I know the forest is being worked on and I can’t thank Alta enough trying to add it to quest and I really appreciate the effort they put into this game(especially for the pc version, which I don’t have😢)

And I mean this literally, nobody is working on content for A Township Tale to instead work on Project 2: Reave. And whilst that’s good and all, it leaves the future of not only the addition of the Forest update but also planned additions in general uncertain. There are still people working in the Backend and some updates like the Revive update and the Blacksmithing tutorial are total improvements and I’ve had so much fun revisiting the game, especially the journey to the Climbing Tower, something I was begging for and upset about in the original review. Ultimately the game is dead, Alta said that the rest of the missing content updates will come to Quest eventually, but that seems unlikely. Will I recommend this game to ATT newcomers? Honestly, no. You can come to your own conclusion, but if I knew this at launch I likely wouldn’t have wanted the game anymore. we need to come  together and tell Alta we aren’t happy. Whilst Reave looks cool, leaving the dedicated and loving community of A Township Tale, a community they’ve been fostering since 2016, a whole 9 years ago is a bad decision. I will up my initial rating down to 1 star due to Alta’s disinterest in finishing the game.

It’s worth buying and playing. But sadly, the game is almost dead. The low amount of players consist of half new players, and half veterans. A lot of att players are people who started playing years ago when the standard was lower for games. Don’t get me wrong, this game is really fun, but it is very hard to learn how to play. There used to be a tutorial in the game that did a decent job at explaining the mechanics but now the tutorial doesn’t work. Which isn’t a completely bad thing because it gives players the opportunity to figure out how to survive on their own and that is the fun part. Nothing in this game is common sense. You have to already know what you’re doing before you do it because most actions in the game are impractical, unclear, and confusing. For example, It took me about 15 minutes just to figure out how to set up the woodworking table. It also took me a while to find out how to make weapons and tools in the smithery. It’s not a terrible game, but it’s not all that great. The map is beautiful but it does desperately need to be updated. There are not enough signs and it’s easy to get lost. it looks very flat and it has few details but the sunsets and sky visuals make up for it. The survival aspects and mechanics are good. This game makes you feel like a medieval explorer. My favorite thing to do in this game is cook food. There is a wide range of food you can cook. Another good aspect of the game is socializing. Its a good game to play with others due to the fact that it’s so funny. I have had countless laughs and deep conversations with people on this game and made some good friends. The worst thing about this game is that the developers have pretty much abandoned it. This had potential to be a great game with just a few tweaks but hopefully their focus on Reave pays off. In the future, I aspire to learn how to make vr games so I can make one like this but not exactly like this.

The game is amazing and was thriving a few years ago, but Alta is focusing on their other game and there have historically been very few updates for the game. If you have a small group of friends that can all play at the same time, then the game is fun. The game is at its best if you have a group of 10-20 people who all play occasionally throughout the day so the server is populated but not cluttered. The game is fantastic and unique, but there's not much content and the game is very grindy.

The game itself is really good full of adventure and different stories. You can create for yourself but sadly at least the quest version of the game hasn’t been getting any updates for a long while which is hindering the expansion of how many players play the game I wish in the future some developers come back and update this game. It’s amazing. Don’t get me wrong, but they could be more

A township tale is a good game.. but it’s dead it’s a small community by the time I write this review, but there are still a small amount of severs still up. I would like it if this game had not died so quickly, it’s an amazing community and its a game where you can use the blacksmith, make potions, cut down trees to make handles for your weapons and more. It’s mostly about exploring, mining and making your weapons. Best VR RPG has lots things you cannot do in other games recommend this game if you want a game to explore and to do things other games don’t let you do.

I was looking for a game like this for ages, until my brother tried it and showed me. I think this game has the highest potential but it is sad the owners stopped working on it. I think the only hope it has is for someone else to buy it and continue work.

reviews.review_title: A Once-Promising VR Experience In Limbo
reviews.review_description:
A Township Tale once stood out as a unique VR multiplayer experience, offering players the chance to collaboratively build and explore a dynamic world. The game's immersive mechanics and communal gameplay garnered a dedicated player base and positive attention within the VR community.​

However, recent developments have cast a shadow over its reputation. Despite earlier promises of expansions, notably the anticipated Forest update, the developers have ceased active development on the game. This stagnation has left the community without the new content and enhancements that were once on the horizon. Compounding the issue, the game remains available for purchase, complete with in-game currency transactions, leading to concerns about the ethics of monetizing a product that no longer receives support. Discussions among players reflect a sense of disillusionment, with sentiments highlighting the frustration of investing in a game that appears to have been abandoned by its creators. ​

In light of these circumstances, potential buyers should approach A Township Tale with caution. While the foundational gameplay offers glimpses of innovation, the lack of ongoing development and support raises questions about the game's future viability. It's advisable to stay informed through official channels and community discussions before making any investment in this title or refer to the other reviews.
I have had this game for awhile now and it is starting to really stick out from other other games in the VR community however this game is dying as there are minimum updates (none).

This game is such a good game and I have had this game for 3 or 4 years, and put over 550 hours of my life in to this game and it had so much potential and still does, but due to alta abandoning this game and laying off their devs, there will be no more content updates and I heard they did this because it was too expensive. but I truly think if a lot of people start to buy this game or buy talems/in game currency and support this game and show them this could be the best game of its time. they will work on it again. Ps: Thanks alta for bringing countless hours of joy into my life, and I used to come home from school be extremely excited to play and I  bet if you still work on this game you can bring the same amount of joy you gave to me to thousands of 
others thanks again peace until next time ;)

reviews.review_title: RIP Township Tale, thanks for memories
reviews.review_description:

                         RIP Township Tale

                              2021-2025
